title: rubus courtoisianus
intro: Rubus courtoisianus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Courtois's raspberry, is a species of raspberry native to the Himalayan region. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Himalayas and parts of Asia, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rubus courtoisianus include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting digestive health, among others. The herbal preparations of Rubus courtoisianus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the leaves and roots of the plant in hot water or a solvent.
